---
title: "Networking and Distributed Systems"
---

# Networking and Distributed Systems

Range: `0771-0886`.

Use the summary table for scanning. Each row links to the detailed anchored entry below.

| Sigil | Term                               | Summary                                                                                                                                |
| ----- | ---------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 0771  | [accept](#rune-0771)               | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                          |
| 0772  | [acknowledgment](#rune-0772)       | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                |
| 0773  | [anycast](#rune-0773)              | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                |
| 0774  | [API request](#rune-0774)          | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                |
| 0775  | [API response](#rune-0775)         | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                        |
| 0776  | [availability zone](#rune-0776)    | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                |
| 0777  | [backoff {Net}](#rune-0777)        | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                          |
| 0778  | [backpressure](#rune-0778)         | Resistance pushed upstream when demand outruns capacity. Without it, overload spreads faster than truth.                               |
| 0779  | [bandwidth {Net}](#rune-0779)      | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                        |
| 0780  | [bind](#rune-0780)                 | A fastening word; it joins a name, port, address, or variable to a concrete referent.                                                  |
| 0781  | [broadcast](#rune-0781)            | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                |
| 0782  | [broker {Net}](#rune-0782)         | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                        |
| 0783  | [CDN](#rune-0783)                  | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                        |
| 0784  | [certificate {Net}](#rune-0784)    | A delegated-trust word; it lets one party assert another's identity by signed authority.                                               |
| 0785  | [checksum handoff](#rune-0785)     | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                        |
| 0786  | [client {Net}](#rune-0786)         | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                |
| 0787  | [client timeout](#rune-0787)       | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                               |
| 0788  | [cluster {Net}](#rune-0788)        | A many-body word; several machines are treated as one operational unit.                                                                |
| 0789  | [connect](#rune-0789)              | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                               |
| 0790  | [connection pool](#rune-0790)      | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                               |
| 0791  | [consensus](#rune-0791)            | A many-machine law-word; it creates shared order without a single unquestioned body.                                                   |
| 0792  | [consistency level](#rune-0792)    | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                               |
| 0793  | [control plane {Net}](#rune-0793)  | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                        |
| 0794  | [CRC](#rune-0794)                  | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                          |
| 0795  | [datagram](#rune-0795)             | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                        |
| 0796  | [deadline](#rune-0796)             | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                               |
| 0797  | [destination](#rune-0797)          | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                               |
| 0798  | [discovery](#rune-0798)            | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                          |
| 0799  | [DNS](#rune-0799)                  | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                        |
| 0800  | [DNS record](#rune-0800)           | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                          |
| 0801  | [edge {Net}](#rune-0801)           | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                        |
| 0802  | [egress](#rune-0802)               | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                        |
| 0803  | [election](#rune-0803)             | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                |
| 0804  | [endpoint](#rune-0804)             | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                        |
| 0805  | [eventual consistency](#rune-0805) | Agreement postponed rather than denied. Shadow: stale reads treated as if they were fresh truth.                                       |
| 0806  | [failover](#rune-0806)             | A continuity word; authority or traffic moves when the current holder fails.                                                           |
| 0807  | [fan-out {Net}](#rune-0807)        | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                               |
| 0808  | [firewall](#rune-0808)             | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                               |
| 0809  | [follower](#rune-0809)             | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                               |
| 0810  | [forwarding](#rune-0810)           | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                |
| 0811  | [frame {Net}](#rune-0811)          | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                |
| 0812  | [gateway {Net}](#rune-0812)        | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                               |
| 0813  | [gossip](#rune-0813)               | A rumor-propagation word; state spreads by repeated local exchange.                                                                    |
| 0814  | [gRPC](#rune-0814)                 | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                          |
| 0815  | [handshake](#rune-0815)            | A mutual-recognition word used to establish parameters, trust, or shared readiness.                                                    |
| 0816  | [health check](#rune-0816)         | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                          |
| 0817  | [heartbeat {Net}](#rune-0817)      | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                          |
| 0818  | [hop](#rune-0818)                  | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                |
| 0819  | [HTTP](#rune-0819)                 | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                          |
| 0820  | [HTTPS](#rune-0820)                | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                |
| 0821  | [idempotency key](#rune-0821)      | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                               |
| 0822  | [ingress](#rune-0822)              | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                |
| 0823  | [jitter {Net}](#rune-0823)         | A timing-noise word; repeated operations arrive with unstable spacing.                                                                 |
| 0824  | [keepalive](#rune-0824)            | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                               |
| 0825  | [latency](#rune-0825)              | The delay before useful work reaches the caller. Latency is what users feel even when throughput graphs look flattering.               |
| 0826  | [leader](#rune-0826)               | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                        |
| 0827  | [lease](#rune-0827)                | A temporary-authority word; a resource is held only for a bounded duration.                                                            |
| 0828  | [load balancer](#rune-0828)        | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                          |
| 0829  | [loss](#rune-0829)                 | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                        |
| 0830  | [mailbox](#rune-0830)              | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                          |
| 0831  | [membership](#rune-0831)           | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                          |
| 0832  | [mesh {Net}](#rune-0832)           | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                        |
| 0833  | [message queue](#rune-0833)        | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                               |
| 0834  | [mTLS {Net}](#rune-0834)           | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                          |
| 0835  | [multicast](#rune-0835)            | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                               |
| 0836  | [NAT](#rune-0836)                  | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                |
| 0837  | [network partition](#rune-0837)    | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                          |
| 0838  | [node {Net}](#rune-0838)           | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                               |
| 0839  | [origin](#rune-0839)               | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                          |
| 0840  | [packet](#rune-0840)               | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                          |
| 0841  | [packet loss {Net}](#rune-0841)    | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                        |
| 0842  | [peer](#rune-0842)                 | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                          |
| 0843  | [pipeline depth](#rune-0843)       | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                |
| 0844  | [port {Net}](#rune-0844)           | A contact word; a defined point of communication or adaptation.                                                                        |
| 0845  | [proxy {Net}](#rune-0845)          | A stand-in word; one body speaks or receives on behalf of another.                                                                     |
| 0846  | [publish-subscribe](#rune-0846)    | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                               |
| 0847  | [QUIC](#rune-0847)                 | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                               |
| 0848  | [quorum](#rune-0848)               | Not everyone must agree, but enough must. This word turns distributed action into a threshold question instead of a unanimous fantasy. |
| 0849  | [rate limit](#rune-0849)           | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                |
| 0850  | [receiver window](#rune-0850)      | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                        |
| 0851  | [redirect](#rune-0851)             | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                               |
| 0852  | [replica](#rune-0852)              | A mirroring word; state is maintained in more than one place.                                                                          |
| 0853  | [replication](#rune-0853)          | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                               |
| 0854  | [request](#rune-0854)              | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                        |
| 0855  | [request coalescing](#rune-0855)   | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                        |
| 0856  | [response](#rune-0856)             | A returning word; the answer carried back across an invoked boundary.                                                                  |
| 0857  | [retry {Net}](#rune-0857)          | A second-attempt word; failure does not end the ritual if policy allows another try.                                                   |
| 0858  | [reverse proxy](#rune-0858)        | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                               |
| 0859  | [route](#rune-0859)                | A path word; messages or requests are sent by a chosen way rather than all ways.                                                       |
| 0860  | [router {Net}](#rune-0860)         | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                        |
| 0861  | [routing table](#rune-0861)        | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                          |
| 0862  | [RPC](#rune-0862)                  | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                        |
| 0863  | [segment {Net}](#rune-0863)        | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                |
| 0864  | [sequence number](#rune-0864)      | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                               |
| 0865  | [service discovery](#rune-0865)    | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                        |
| 0866  | [service mesh {Net}](#rune-0866)   | A side-channel governance word for service-to-service traffic.                                                                         |
| 0867  | [shard {Net}](#rune-0867)          | A splitting word; one logical body is divided across multiple partitions.                                                              |
| 0868  | [sidecar](#rune-0868)              | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                |
| 0869  | [sliding window](#rune-0869)       | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                               |
| 0870  | [socket {Net}](#rune-0870)         | A conduit word; a live endpoint for network communication.                                                                             |
| 0871  | [source](#rune-0871)               | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                        |
| 0872  | [split-brain {Net}](#rune-0872)    | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                        |
| 0873  | [stream {Net}](#rune-0873)         | A flow word; values arrive over time rather than all at once.                                                                          |
| 0874  | [strong consistency](#rune-0874)   | A sameness word across distance; readers do not see old worlds after new ones commit.                                                  |
| 0875  | [subscription](#rune-0875)         | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                        |
| 0876  | [TCP](#rune-0876)                  | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                |
| 0877  | [timeout {Net}](#rune-0877)        | A patience-bound word; after a duration, waiting is no longer allowed.                                                                 |
| 0878  | [TLS](#rune-0878)                  | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                |
| 0879  | [topic](#rune-0879)                | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                |
| 0880  | [tracing header](#rune-0880)       | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                        |
| 0881  | [UDP](#rune-0881)                  | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                        |
| 0882  | [unicast](#rune-0882)              | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                |
| 0883  | [virtual IP](#rune-0883)           | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                          |
| 0884  | [webhook](#rune-0884)              | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                |
| 0885  | [WebSocket](#rune-0885)            | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                |
| 0886  | [wire format](#rune-0886)          | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                          |

## Entries

### [0771] accept {#rune-0771}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.

### [0772] acknowledgment {#rune-0772}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.

### [0773] anycast {#rune-0773}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.

### [0774] API request {#rune-0774}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.

### [0775] API response {#rune-0775}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.

### [0776] availability zone {#rune-0776}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.

### [0777] backoff {Net} {#rune-0777}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.

### [0778] backpressure {#rune-0778}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Force:** Resistance pushed upstream when demand outruns capacity. Without it, overload spreads faster than truth.

**Pocket gloss:** Push resistance upstream when demand outruns capacity.

### [0779] bandwidth {Net} {#rune-0779}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.

### [0780] bind {#rune-0780}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A fastening word; it joins a name, port, address, or variable to a concrete referent.

### [0781] broadcast {#rune-0781}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.

### [0782] broker {Net} {#rune-0782}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.

### [0783] CDN {#rune-0783}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.

### [0784] certificate {Net} {#rune-0784}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A delegated-trust word; it lets one party assert another's identity by signed authority.

### [0785] checksum handoff {#rune-0785}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.

### [0786] client {Net} {#rune-0786}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.

### [0787] client timeout {#rune-0787}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.

### [0788] cluster {Net} {#rune-0788}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A many-body word; several machines are treated as one operational unit.

### [0789] connect {#rune-0789}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.

### [0790] connection pool {#rune-0790}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.

### [0791] consensus {#rune-0791}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A many-machine law-word; it creates shared order without a single unquestioned body.

**Pocket gloss:** Agreement protocol for shared truth across unreliable nodes.

### [0792] consistency level {#rune-0792}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.

### [0793] control plane {Net} {#rune-0793}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.

### [0794] CRC {#rune-0794}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.

### [0795] datagram {#rune-0795}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.

### [0796] deadline {#rune-0796}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.

### [0797] destination {#rune-0797}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.

### [0798] discovery {#rune-0798}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.

### [0799] DNS {#rune-0799}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.

**Pocket gloss:** Translate names into network-reachable locations.

### [0800] DNS record {#rune-0800}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.

### [0801] edge {Net} {#rune-0801}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.

### [0802] egress {#rune-0802}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.

### [0803] election {#rune-0803}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.

### [0804] endpoint {#rune-0804}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.

**Pocket gloss:** A callable boundary where a service can be reached.

### [0805] eventual consistency {#rune-0805}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Force:** Agreement postponed rather than denied.

**Shadow:** stale reads treated as if they were fresh truth.

**Pocket gloss:** Agreement postponed rather than denied.

### [0806] failover {#rune-0806}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A continuity word; authority or traffic moves when the current holder fails.

**Pocket gloss:** Shift responsibility to a surviving node or path after failure.

### [0807] fan-out {Net} {#rune-0807}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.

### [0808] firewall {#rune-0808}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.

### [0809] follower {#rune-0809}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.

### [0810] forwarding {#rune-0810}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.

### [0811] frame {Net} {#rune-0811}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.

### [0812] gateway {Net} {#rune-0812}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.

### [0813] gossip {#rune-0813}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A rumor-propagation word; state spreads by repeated local exchange.

### [0814] gRPC {#rune-0814}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.

### [0815] handshake {#rune-0815}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A mutual-recognition word used to establish parameters, trust, or shared readiness.

**Pocket gloss:** Opening ritual that establishes trust, parameters, or session state.

### [0816] health check {#rune-0816}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.

### [0817] heartbeat {Net} {#rune-0817}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.

### [0818] hop {#rune-0818}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.

### [0819] HTTP {#rune-0819}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.

**Pocket gloss:** The dominant request-response protocol of the web.

### [0820] HTTPS {#rune-0820}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.

### [0821] idempotency key {#rune-0821}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.

### [0822] ingress {#rune-0822}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.

**Pocket gloss:** Traffic entering a service boundary.

### [0823] jitter {Net} {#rune-0823}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A timing-noise word; repeated operations arrive with unstable spacing.

### [0824] keepalive {#rune-0824}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.

### [0825] latency {#rune-0825}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Force:** The delay before useful work reaches the caller. Latency is what users feel even when throughput graphs look flattering.

**Pocket gloss:** The delay before useful work reaches the caller.

### [0826] leader {#rune-0826}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.

### [0827] lease {#rune-0827}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A temporary-authority word; a resource is held only for a bounded duration.

### [0828] load balancer {#rune-0828}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.

**Pocket gloss:** Distribute incoming work across many backends.

### [0829] loss {#rune-0829}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.

### [0830] mailbox {#rune-0830}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.

### [0831] membership {#rune-0831}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.

### [0832] mesh {Net} {#rune-0832}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.

### [0833] message queue {#rune-0833}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.

### [0834] mTLS {Net} {#rune-0834}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.

**Pocket gloss:** Mutual certificate-based identity proof on both sides of a connection.

### [0835] multicast {#rune-0835}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.

### [0836] NAT {#rune-0836}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.

### [0837] network partition {#rune-0837}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.

**Pocket gloss:** A split network that makes one cluster behave like several worlds.

### [0838] node {Net} {#rune-0838}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.

### [0839] origin {#rune-0839}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.

### [0840] packet {#rune-0840}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.

### [0841] packet loss {Net} {#rune-0841}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.

### [0842] peer {#rune-0842}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.

### [0843] pipeline depth {#rune-0843}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.

### [0844] port {Net} {#rune-0844}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A contact word; a defined point of communication or adaptation.

### [0845] proxy {Net} {#rune-0845}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A stand-in word; one body speaks or receives on behalf of another.

### [0846] publish-subscribe {#rune-0846}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.

### [0847] QUIC {#rune-0847}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.

### [0848] quorum {#rune-0848}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Force:** Not everyone must agree, but enough must. This word turns distributed action into a threshold question instead of a unanimous fantasy.

**Pocket gloss:** Enough agreement to act without requiring unanimity.

### [0849] rate limit {#rune-0849}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.

### [0850] receiver window {#rune-0850}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.

### [0851] redirect {#rune-0851}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.

### [0852] replica {#rune-0852}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A mirroring word; state is maintained in more than one place.

**Pocket gloss:** A copy kept elsewhere for durability, scale, or locality.

### [0853] replication {#rune-0853}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.

### [0854] request {#rune-0854}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.

### [0855] request coalescing {#rune-0855}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.

### [0856] response {#rune-0856}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A returning word; the answer carried back across an invoked boundary.

### [0857] retry {Net} {#rune-0857}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A second-attempt word; failure does not end the ritual if policy allows another try.

### [0858] reverse proxy {#rune-0858}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.

### [0859] route {#rune-0859}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A path word; messages or requests are sent by a chosen way rather than all ways.

**Pocket gloss:** The chosen path by which traffic should travel.

### [0860] router {Net} {#rune-0860}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.

### [0861] routing table {#rune-0861}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.

### [0862] RPC {#rune-0862}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.

**Pocket gloss:** Invoke remote behavior as though it were local, with all the risks that implies.

### [0863] segment {Net} {#rune-0863}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.

### [0864] sequence number {#rune-0864}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.

### [0865] service discovery {#rune-0865}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.

### [0866] service mesh {Net} {#rune-0866}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A side-channel governance word for service-to-service traffic.

### [0867] shard {Net} {#rune-0867}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A splitting word; one logical body is divided across multiple partitions.

### [0868] sidecar {#rune-0868}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.

### [0869] sliding window {#rune-0869}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.

### [0870] socket {Net} {#rune-0870}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A conduit word; a live endpoint for network communication.

**Pocket gloss:** A network communication endpoint bound to protocol and address.

### [0871] source {#rune-0871}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.

### [0872] split-brain {Net} {#rune-0872}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.

### [0873] stream {Net} {#rune-0873}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A flow word; values arrive over time rather than all at once.

### [0874] strong consistency {#rune-0874}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A sameness word across distance; readers do not see old worlds after new ones commit.

### [0875] subscription {#rune-0875}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.

### [0876] TCP {#rune-0876}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.

**Pocket gloss:** Ordered reliable stream transport with congestion control.

### [0877] timeout {Net} {#rune-0877}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A patience-bound word; after a duration, waiting is no longer allowed.

### [0878] TLS {#rune-0878}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.

### [0879] topic {#rune-0879}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.

### [0880] tracing header {#rune-0880}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.

### [0881] UDP {#rune-0881}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.

### [0882] unicast {#rune-0882}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.

### [0883] virtual IP {#rune-0883}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.

### [0884] webhook {#rune-0884}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.

**Pocket gloss:** An outbound callback triggered by an event.

### [0885] WebSocket {#rune-0885}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.

### [0886] wire format {#rune-0886}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.
